title: Savory Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: A rich and flavorful Moroccan dish featuring lamb, cardamom, and a blend of spices, resulting in a fatty and molten texture. This recipe is perfect for special occasions and is sure to impress your guests.

ingredients.name: lamb shoulder
ingredients.notes: boneless and cut into 2 inch pieces

instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the lamb pieces and cook until they are browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the crushed cardamom pods, cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken broth, honey, and salt and pepper to taste, and bring the mixture to a boil.
Cover the tagine or Dutch oven and transfer it to the oven, where it will cook for 2.5 hours, or until the lamb is very tender.
Remove the tagine or Dutch oven from the oven and let it cool slightly before serving.
Serve the lamb and sauce over couscous or rice, garnished with chopped fresh herbs and a dollop of yogurt if desired.
